sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 65

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 65:1
items.html:
(to the question) The measure of the Yesod
The Malchut is called Revi'it [a fourth], in the secret of the verse "a fourth of a hin." This is because she is the fourth letter of the four-letter Name; and the principal reason is that "fourth" carries the meaning of Zivug, and since the place of the Zivug is in the Nukva, she is called Revi'it. And it is known that in the world of Tikun [correction], the Yesod was made the place for the Zivug, in place of the Malchut in the Partzufim of AK. Therefore the Yesod, too, received this name, and is called Rova [a fourth part], in the secret of the verse "and the number of the fourth part of Israel." And there is another reason: the measure of the Guf is four covenants — meaning there are in it four places of Zivug, in the secret of the four discernments of Aviut in the Masach — and therefore every Yesod is discerned as one fourth. For the Partzuf is not completed until it has the four discernments of the Zivug in completeness, namely: the level of NHY, the level of HGT, the level of HBD of Neshama, and the level of HBD of Chaya; and then it is completed, as is known. Therefore the measure of the Yesod of every level is discerned as one quarter of the completeness of the Guf. (p. 769, Item 52)
